summary: AI Prediction module (rule-engine + sklearn hybrid) tested end-to-end. Backend: 10/10 pytest passed covering schema (engine_version=prediction-v1-hybrid, 6 failure_types with all required fields incl. model_probability_percent/rule_probability_percent), single-failure endpoint, 400 for unknown type, timeline growth (count increments after successive /predictions calls), failure_type filtering on timeline, failing telemetry -> all six 'critical'/'high' with prob>=70% and coherent reason strings, nominal telemetry -> info/low <20%, unauth 401/403, cross-org 404. Startup log shows 'training prediction model for {ssd,fan,cpu_thermal,battery,memory,network}'. Frontend: AI Prediction tab renders 6 prediction-card-* elements with prediction-probability-*, prediction-confidence-* and prediction-severity-* (fixed – see below), Recompute button works, range selectors (1h/24h/7d/30d) switch without page reload, detail panel + timeline chart + reason + recommendation + historical predictions table all render correctly.

issues: Recharts warning 'width(-1) height(-1)' fires on initial mount when sparkline container hasn't measured yet — cosmetic only, chart renders correctly after first paint

action_items: None blocking. Optional: guard PredictionCard sparkline ResponsiveContainer with a minHeight or skip until series.length>=2 to silence Recharts width(-1) warning.
critical_code_review_comments: PredictionPanel.jsx was passing `data-testid` to StatBadge, but StatBadge only forwards a `testId` prop -> severity badges had NO data-testid at runtime. Fixed by testing agent (single-line edit to use testId prop). Consider making StatBadge accept `data-testid` directly (spread {...rest}) to avoid this trap for future callers.
engine.py blends rule (60%) + model (40%), with rule-dominant blend (75/25) when rule>=70 and coverage down-weighting <25% — looks well designed and matches the review spec.

predictions.py _persist_snapshot inserts a doc per GET /predictions call. Under heavy UI polling this collection can grow unbounded; recommend a TTL index (e.g. ts>30d) or capped collection. Not blocking for MVP.

predictions_timeline query uses ISO string ts with $gte on ISO string comparison — works because ISO 8601 sorts lexicographically, but relies on consistent timezone suffix. Current code always writes UTC isoformat so this is safe.
features.py memory: `'thermal_throttling' in m` / `'memory_leak_detected' in m` treat presence-of-key as coverage; harmless but note that False-valued keys still count. OK for now.
seed_data_creation: Enrolled 2 test devices via /api/enrollment/codes+enroll (pred-failing, pred-nominal) and injected failing/nominal latest_metrics directly into mongo devices collection (test_database). Reused existing device 722cc76b049541ea9ddb2ee239d248d6 for UI smoke by writing failing metrics into it. All artifacts live under org 0b506039-ccbc-4611-955c-d190b2cc8135.
context_for_next_testing_agent: AI Prediction module fully validated. If iterating: pytest file /app/backend/tests/test_predictions.py auto-enrolls its own devices and injects metrics via pymongo so it is self-contained. Frontend test used data-testid selectors: tab-ai-prediction, ai-prediction-panel, prediction-card-{type}, prediction-severity-{type}, prediction-probability-{type}, prediction-confidence-{type}, prediction-refresh, prediction-range-{1h|24h|7d|30d}, prediction-detail-panel, prediction-history-list. Phase-6 flows from iteration_1 were not re-tested per instruction.
